[[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun Stone Chamber}}]] '''Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' (越塚御門古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It has been designated a {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
The discovery of the tumulus was announced on {{ill|December 9, 2010|JA|12月9日}}, and it is situated adjacent to the {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}}. Based on descriptions in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, it is believed to be the probable burial site of {{ill|Princess Ōta|ja|大田皇女}}, a member of the Imperial family from the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}.
== Discovery == [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] In mid-October 2010, during the process of backfilling the excavation site around the Kengoshizuka Kofun, {{ill|Shinji Saikō|ja|西光慎治}}, a technician with the Asuka Village Board of Education, noticed a one-meter stone buried aqqroximately 20 meters southeast of the Kengoshizuka Kofun. The stone showed clear evidence of human modification, prompting further excavation that led to the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20101214104644/http://www.iza.ne.jp/news/newsarticle/natnews/topics/472754/ "Unexpected Major Discovery Near Kengoshizuka Kofun"], Sankei Shimbun, December 9, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Response from the Imperial Household Agency == Despite the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has not revised its designation of the tumulus near the {{ill|Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja|車木ケンノウ古墳}} (currently identified by the agency as the mausoleum of {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇}}) as the burial site of Ōta no Himemiko. The agency cites discrepancies between the archaeological findings and the Nihon Shoki's account, which states that {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} did not impose forced labor on the populace to construct the stone chamber.<ref>[http://mainichi.jp/enta/art/news/20101210ddm012040038000c.html "Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun: Discovered as Described in the Nihon Shoki; Debate Over Imperial Mausoleum Designations Accelerates"], Mainichi Shimbun, December 10, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' - In 2014 (Heisei 26), the previously designated Historic Site "Kengoshizuka Kofun" was expanded to include the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, and the designation was renamed "Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun."<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1945|牽牛子塚古墳・越塚御門古墳}}</ref>

The discovery of the tumulus was announced on {{ill|December 9, 2010|JA|12月9日}}, and it is situated adjacent to the {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}}. == Discovery == [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] In mid-October 2010, during the process of backfilling the excavation site around the Kengoshizuka Kofun, {{ill|Shinji Saikō|ja|西光慎治}}, a technician with the Asuka Village Board of Education, noticed a one-meter stone buried a11roximately 20 meters southeast of the Kengoshizuka Kofun. The stone showed clear evidence of human modification, prompting further excavation that led to the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20101214104644/http://www.iza.ne.jp/news/newsarticle/natnews/topics/472754/ "Unexpected Major Discovery Near Kengoshizuka Kofun"], Sankei Shimbun, December 9, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Response from the Imperial Household Agency == Despite the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has not revised its designation of the tumulus near the {{ill|Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja|車木ケンノウ古墳}} (currently identified by the agency as the mausoleum of {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇}}) as the burial site of Ōta no Himemiko. The agency cites discrepancies between the archaeological findings and the Nihon Shoki's account, which states that {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} did not impose forced labor on the populace to construct the stone chamber.<ref>[http://mainichi.jp/enta/art/news/20101210ddm012040038000c.html "Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun: Discovered as Described in the Nihon Shoki; Debate Over Imperial Mausoleum Designations Accelerates"], Mainichi Shimbun, December 10, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' - In 2014 (Heisei 26), the previously designated Historic Site "Kengoshizuka Kofun" was expanded to include the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, and the designation was renamed "Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun."<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1945|牽牛子塚古墳・越塚御門古墳}}</ref>

[[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun Stone Chamber}}]] '''Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' (越塚御門古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It has been designated a {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
The discovery of the tumulus was announced on {{ill|December 9, 2010|JA|12月9日}}, and it is situated adjacent to the {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}}. Based on descriptions in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, it is believed to be the probable burial site of {{ill|Princess Ōta|ja|大田皇女}}, a member of the Imperial family from the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}.
== Discovery == [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] In mid-October 2010, during the process of backfilling the excavation site around the Kengoshizuka Kofun, {{ill|Shinji Saikō|ja|西光慎治}}, a technician with the Asuka Village Board of Education, noticed a one-meter stone buried a11roximately 20 meters southeast of the Kengoshizuka Kofun. The stone showed clear evidence of human modification, prompting further excavation that led to the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20101214104644/http://www.iza.ne.jp/news/newsarticle/natnews/topics/472754/ "Unexpected Major Discovery Near Kengoshizuka Kofun"], Sankei Shimbun, December 9, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Response from the Imperial Household Agency == Despite the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has not revised its designation of the tumulus near the {{ill|Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja|車木ケンノウ古墳}} (currently identified by the agency as the mausoleum of {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇}}) as the burial site of Ōta no Himemiko. The agency cites discrepancies between the archaeological findings and the Nihon Shoki's account, which states that {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} did not impose forced labor on the populace to construct the stone chamber.<ref>[http://mainichi.jp/enta/art/news/20101210ddm012040038000c.html "Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun: Discovered as Described in the Nihon Shoki; Debate Over Imperial Mausoleum Designations Accelerates"], Mainichi Shimbun, December 10, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' - In 2014 (Heisei 26), the previously designated Historic Site "Kengoshizuka Kofun" was expanded to include the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, and the designation was renamed "Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun."<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1945|牽牛子塚古墳・越塚御門古墳}}</ref>

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 1]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun Stone Chamber}}]] '''Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' (越塚御門古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It has been designated a {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
The discovery of the tumulus was announced on {{ill|December 9, 2010|JA|12月9日}}, and it is situated adjacent to the {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}}. Based on descriptions in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, it is believed to be the probable burial site of {{ill|Princess Ōta|ja|大田皇女}}, a member of the Imperial family from the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}.
== Discovery == [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] In mid-October 2010, during the process of backfilling the excavation site around the Kengoshizuka Kofun, {{ill|Shinji Saikō|ja|西光慎治}}, a technician with the Asuka Village Board of Education, noticed a one-meter stone buried [[Category:pp error]]approximately 20 meters southeast of the Kengoshizuka Kofun. The stone showed clear evidence of human modification, prompting further excavation that led to the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20101214104644/http://www.iza.ne.jp/news/newsarticle/natnews/topics/472754/ "Unexpected Major Discovery Near Kengoshizuka Kofun"], Sankei Shimbun, December 9, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Response from the Imperial Household Agency == Despite the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has not revised its designation of the tumulus near the {{ill|Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja|車木ケンノウ古墳}} (currently identified by the agency as the mausoleum of {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇}}) as the burial site of Ōta no Himemiko. The agency cites discrepancies between the archaeological findings and the Nihon Shoki's account, which states that {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} did not impose forced labor on the populace to construct the stone chamber.<ref>[http://mainichi.jp/enta/art/news/20101210ddm012040038000c.html "Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun: Discovered as Described in the Nihon Shoki; Debate Over Imperial Mausoleum Designations Accelerates"], Mainichi Shimbun, December 10, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' - In 2014 (Heisei 26), the previously designated Historic Site "Kengoshizuka Kofun" was expanded to include the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, and the designation was renamed "Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun."<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1945|牽牛子塚古墳・越塚御門古墳}}</ref>
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1945|牽牛子塚古墳・越塚御門古墳}}

[[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun Stone Chamber}}]] '''Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' (越塚御門古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It has been designated a {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
The discovery of the tumulus was announced on {{ill|December 9, 2010|JA|12月9日}}, and it is situated adjacent to the {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}}. Based on descriptions in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, it is believed to be the probable burial site of {{ill|Princess Ōta|ja|大田皇女}}, a member of the Imperial family from the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}.
== Discovery == [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] In mid-October 2010, during the process of backfilling the excavation site around the Kengoshizuka Kofun, {{ill|Shinji Saikō|ja|西光慎治}}, a technician with the Asuka Village Board of Education, noticed a one-meter stone buried [[Category:pp error]]approximately 20 meters southeast of the Kengoshizuka Kofun. The stone showed clear evidence of human modification, prompting further excavation that led to the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20101214104644/http://www.iza.ne.jp/news/newsarticle/natnews/topics/472754/ "Unexpected Major Discovery Near Kengoshizuka Kofun"], Sankei Shimbun, December 9, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Response from the Imperial Household Agency == Despite the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has not revised its designation of the tumulus near the {{ill|Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja|車木ケンノウ古墳}} (currently identified by the agency as the mausoleum of {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇}}) as the burial site of Ōta no Himemiko. The agency cites discrepancies between the archaeological findings and the Nihon Shoki's account, which states that {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} did not impose forced labor on the populace to construct the stone chamber.<ref>[http://mainichi.jp/enta/art/news/20101210ddm012040038000c.html "Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun: Discovered as Described in the Nihon Shoki; Debate Over Imperial Mausoleum Designations Accelerates"], Mainichi Shimbun, December 10, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' - In 2014 (Heisei 26), the previously designated Historic Site "Kengoshizuka Kofun" was expanded to include the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, and the designation was renamed "Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun."<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1945|牽牛子塚古墳・越塚御門古墳}}</ref>

[[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun Stone Chamber}}]] '''Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' (越塚御門古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It has been designated a {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
The discovery of the tumulus was announced on {{ill|December 9, 2010|JA|12月9日}}, and it is situated adjacent to the {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}}. Based on descriptions in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, it is believed to be the probable burial site of {{ill|Princess Ōta|ja|大田皇女}}, a member of the Imperial family from the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}.
== Discovery == [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] In mid-October 2010, during the process of backfilling the excavation site around the Kengoshizuka Kofun, {{ill|Shinji Saikō|ja|西光慎治}}, a technician with the Asuka Village Board of Education, noticed a one-meter stone buried [[Category:pp error]]approximately 20 meters southeast of the Kengoshizuka Kofun. The stone showed clear evidence of human modification, prompting further excavation that led to the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20101214104644/http://www.iza.ne.jp/news/newsarticle/natnews/topics/472754/ "Unexpected Major Discovery Near Kengoshizuka Kofun"], Sankei Shimbun, December 9, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Response from the Imperial Household Agency == Despite the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has not revised its designation of the tumulus near the {{ill|Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja|車木ケンノウ古墳}} (currently identified by the agency as the mausoleum of {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇}}) as the burial site of Ōta no Himemiko. The agency cites discrepancies between the archaeological findings and the Nihon Shoki's account, which states that {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} did not impose forced labor on the populace to construct the stone chamber.<ref>[http://mainichi.jp/enta/art/news/20101210ddm012040038000c.html "Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun: Discovered as Described in the Nihon Shoki; Debate Over Imperial Mausoleum Designations Accelerates"], Mainichi Shimbun, December 10, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' - In 2014 (Heisei 26), the previously designated Historic Site "Kengoshizuka Kofun" was expanded to include the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, and the designation was renamed "Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun."<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1945|牽牛子塚古墳・越塚御門古墳}}</ref>
== References ==
{{reflist}}

== Response from the Imperial Household Agency == Despite the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has not revised its designation of the tumulus near the {{ill|Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja|車木ケンノウ古墳}} (currently identified by the agency as the mausoleum of {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇}}) as the burial site of Ōta no Himemiko. The agency cites discrepancies between the archaeological findings and the Nihon Shoki's account, which states that {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} did not impose forced labor on the populace to construct the stone chamber.<ref>[http://mainichi.jp/enta/art/news/20101210ddm012040038000c.html "Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun: Discovered as Described in the Nihon Shoki; Debate Over Imperial Mausoleum Designations Accelerates"], Mainichi Shimbun, December 10, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' - In 2014 (Heisei 26), the previously designated Historic Site "Kengoshizuka Kofun" was expanded to include the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, and the designation was renamed "Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun."<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1945|牽牛子塚古墳・越塚御門古墳}}</ref>

revision.text:
[[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun Stone Chamber}}]] '''Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' (越塚御門古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It has been designated a {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
The discovery of the tumulus was announced on {{ill|December 9, 2010|JA|12月9日}}, and it is situated adjacent to the {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}}. Based on descriptions in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, it is believed to be the probable burial site of {{ill|Princess Ōta|ja|大田皇女}}, a member of the Imperial family from the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}.
== Discovery == [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] In mid-October 2010, during the process of backfilling the excavation site around the Kengoshizuka Kofun, {{ill|Shinji Saikō|ja|西光慎治}}, a technician with the Asuka Village Board of Education, noticed a one-meter stone buried approximately 20 meters southeast of the Kengoshizuka Kofun. The stone showed clear evidence of human modification, prompting further excavation that led to the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20101214104644/http://www.iza.ne.jp/news/newsarticle/natnews/topics/472754/ "Unexpected Major Discovery Near Kengoshizuka Kofun"], Sankei Shimbun, December 9, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Response from the Imperial Household Agency == Despite the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has not revised its designation of the tumulus near the {{ill|Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja|車木ケンノウ古墳}} (currently identified by the agency as the mausoleum of {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇}}) as the burial site of Ōta no Himemiko. The agency cites discrepancies between the archaeological findings and the Nihon Shoki's account, which states that {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} did not impose forced labor on the populace to construct the stone chamber.<ref>[http://mainichi.jp/enta/art/news/20101210ddm012040038000c.html "Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun: Discovered as Described in the Nihon Shoki; Debate Over Imperial Mausoleum Designations Accelerates"], Mainichi Shimbun, December 10, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' - In 2014 (Heisei 26), the previously designated Historic Site "Kengoshizuka Kofun" was expanded to include the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, and the designation was renamed "Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun."<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1945|牽牛子塚古墳・越塚御門古墳}}</ref>
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1945|牽牛子塚古墳・越塚御門古墳}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

The stone showed clear evidence of human modification, prompting further excavation that led to the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20101214104644/http://www.iza.ne.jp/news/newsarticle/natnews/topics/472754/ "Unexpected Major Discovery Near Kengoshizuka Kofun"], Sankei Shimbun, December 9, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Response from the Imperial Household Agency == Despite the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} has not revised its designation of the tumulus near the {{ill|Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja|車木ケンノウ古墳|12=draft}} (currently identified by the agency as the mausoleum of {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇|12=draft}}) as the burial site of Ōta no Himemiko. The agency cites discrepancies between the archaeological findings and the Nihon Shoki's account, which states that {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft}} did not impose forced labor on the populace to construct the stone chamber.<ref>[http://mainichi.jp/enta/art/news/20101210ddm012040038000c.html "Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun: Discovered as Described in the Nihon Shoki; Debate Over Imperial Mausoleum Designations Accelerates"], Mainichi Shimbun, December 10, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' - In 2014 (Heisei 26), the previously designated Historic Site "Kengoshizuka Kofun" was expanded to include the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, and the designation was renamed "Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun."<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1945|牽牛子塚古墳・越塚御門古墳}}</ref>
== References ==

{{Japanese-history-stub}}

12 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:越塚御門古墳 up to rev 101914259
revision.text:
[[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|越塚御門古墳 石槨}}]] '''越塚御門古墳'''（こしつかごもんこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]大字越にある[[古墳]]。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。
[[2010年]][[12月9日]]にその発見が発表された。[[牽牛子塚古墳]]に隣接する古墳であり、『[[日本書紀]]』の記述から[[飛鳥時代]]の[[皇族]]である[[大田皇女]]の墓である可能性が高いと言われている。
== 発見の経緯 == [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石槨俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石槨展開図}}]] 2010年10月中旬、牽牛子塚古墳周辺の発掘現場を埋め戻す最中、明日香村教育委員会の技師である[[西光慎治]]は、古墳の南東約20メートルに埋まっていた1メートルほどの石に人為的に割れた痕跡があったため、気になってその周辺を発掘したことが発見に繋がった<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20101214104644/http://www.iza.ne.jp/news/newsarticle/natnews/topics/472754/ 牽牛子塚古墳 「こんな足もとに」思わぬ大発見 」：イザ！ 産経新聞2010年12月9日] 2010年12月閲覧</ref>。
== 宮内庁の反応 == [[宮内庁]]は越塚御門古墳の発見後も、[[車木ケンノウ古墳]]（宮内庁により[[斉明天皇]]の陵とされている）付近にある墳土を大田皇女の陵墓とする指定を変更する姿勢を見せていない。理由として、『日本書紀』の「[[天智天皇|中大兄皇子]]が石槨を造る労役を民に課さなかった」という記述と一致しないためとしている<ref>[http://mainichi.jp/enta/art/news/20101210ddm012040038000c.html 越塚御門古墳：日本書紀通りに発見 陵墓見直し論、加速も 毎日新聞2010年12月10日] 2010年12月閲覧</ref>。
== 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 牽牛子塚古墳・越塚御門古墳 - 2014年（平成26年）3月18日に既指定の史跡「牽牛子塚古墳」に越塚御門古墳を追加して、指定名称を「牽牛子塚古墳・越塚御門古墳」に変更<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1945|牽牛子塚古墳・越塚御門古墳}}</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1945|牽牛子塚古墳・越塚御門古墳}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こしつかこもんこふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:明日香村の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県にある国指定の史跡]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun]][[ja:越塚御門古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Asuka|qid=Q752397}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11636455]] Wikidata name: Koshitsuka Gomon Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11636455]] Wikidata name: Koshitsuka Gomon Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun Stone Chamber}}]] '''Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' (越塚御門古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It has been designated a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}.
The discovery of the tumulus was announced on {{ill|December 9, 2010|JA|12月9日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 9, 2010|qq=|1=December 9|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2304}}, and it is situated adjacent to the [[:en:Kengoshizuka Kofun|Kengoshizuka Kofun]]. Based on descriptions in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], it is believed to be the probable burial site of [[:en:Princess Ōta|Princess Ōta]], a member of the Imperial family from the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]].
== Discovery == [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] In mid-October 2010, during the process of backfilling the excavation site around the Kengoshizuka Kofun, {{ill|Shinji Saikō|ja|西光慎治|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji Saikō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a technician with the Asuka Village Board of Education, noticed a one-meter stone buried approximately 20 meters southeast of the Kengoshizuka Kofun. The stone showed clear evidence of human modification, prompting further excavation that led to the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20101214104644/http://www.iza.ne.jp/news/newsarticle/natnews/topics/472754/ "Unexpected Major Discovery Near Kengoshizuka Kofun"], Sankei Shimbun, December 9, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Response from the Imperial Household Agency == Despite the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has not revised its designation of the tumulus near the {{ill|Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja|車木ケンノウ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramoki no Misasagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (currently identified by the agency as the mausoleum of [[:en:Empress Kōgyoku|Empress Saimei]]) as the burial site of Ōta no Himemiko. The agency cites discrepancies between the archaeological findings and the Nihon Shoki's account, which states that [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] did not impose forced labor on the populace to construct the stone chamber.<ref>[http://mainichi.jp/enta/art/news/20101210ddm012040038000c.html "Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun: Discovered as Described in the Nihon Shoki; Debate Over Imperial Mausoleum Designations Accelerates"], Mainichi Shimbun, December 10, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' - In 2014 (Heisei 26), the previously designated Historic Site "Kengoshizuka Kofun" was expanded to include the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, and the designation was renamed "Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun."<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1945|牽牛子塚古墳・越塚御門古墳}}</ref>

{{reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun]][[ja:越塚御門古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Asuka|qid=Q752397}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11636455]] Wikidata name: Koshitsuka Gomon Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11636455]] Wikidata name: Koshitsuka Gomon Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun Stone Chamber}}]] '''Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' (越塚御門古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It has been designated a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}.
The discovery of the tumulus was announced on {{ill|December 9, 2010|JA|12月9日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 9, 2010|qq=|1=December 9|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2304}}, and it is situated adjacent to the [[:en:Kengoshizuka Kofun|Kengoshizuka Kofun]]. Based on descriptions in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], it is believed to be the probable burial site of [[:en:Princess Ōta|Princess Ōta]], a member of the Imperial family from the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]].
== Discovery == [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] In mid-October 2010, during the process of backfilling the excavation site around the Kengoshizuka Kofun, {{ill|Shinji Saikō|ja|西光慎治|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji Saikō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a technician with the Asuka Village Board of Education, noticed a one-meter stone buried approximately 20 meters southeast of the Kengoshizuka Kofun. The stone showed clear evidence of human modification, prompting further excavation that led to the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20101214104644/http://www.iza.ne.jp/news/newsarticle/natnews/topics/472754/ "Unexpected Major Discovery Near Kengoshizuka Kofun"], Sankei Shimbun, December 9, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Response from the Imperial Household Agency == Despite the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has not revised its designation of the tumulus near the {{ill|Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja|車木ケンノウ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramoki no Misasagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (currently identified by the agency as the mausoleum of [[:en:Empress Kōgyoku|Empress Saimei]]) as the burial site of Ōta no Himemiko. The agency cites discrepancies between the archaeological findings and the Nihon Shoki's account, which states that [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] did not impose forced labor on the populace to construct the stone chamber.<ref>[http://mainichi.jp/enta/art/news/20101210ddm012040038000c.html "Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun: Discovered as Described in the Nihon Shoki; Debate Over Imperial Mausoleum Designations Accelerates"], Mainichi Shimbun, December 10, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' - In 2014 (Heisei 26), the previously designated Historic Site "Kengoshizuka Kofun" was expanded to include the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, and the designation was renamed "Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun."<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1945|牽牛子塚古墳・越塚御門古墳}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun]][[ja:越塚御門古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11636455]] Wikidata name: Koshitsuka Gomon Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11636455]] Wikidata name: Koshitsuka Gomon Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun Stone Chamber}}]] '''Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' (越塚御門古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It has been designated a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}.
The discovery of the tumulus was announced on {{ill|December 9, 2010|JA|12月9日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 9, 2010|1=December 9|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2304}}, and it is situated adjacent to the [[:en:Kengoshizuka Kofun|Kengoshizuka Kofun]]. Based on descriptions in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], it is believed to be the probable burial site of [[:en:Princess Ōta|Princess Ōta]], a member of the Imperial family from the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]].
== Discovery == [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] In mid-October 2010, during the process of backfilling the excavation site around the Kengoshizuka Kofun, {{ill|Shinji Saikō|ja|西光慎治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji Saikō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a technician with the Asuka Village Board of Education, noticed a one-meter stone buried approximately 20 meters southeast of the Kengoshizuka Kofun. The stone showed clear evidence of human modification, prompting further excavation that led to the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20101214104644/http://www.iza.ne.jp/news/newsarticle/natnews/topics/472754/ "Unexpected Major Discovery Near Kengoshizuka Kofun"], Sankei Shimbun, December 9, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Response from the Imperial Household Agency == Despite the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has not revised its designation of the tumulus near the {{ill|Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja|車木ケンノウ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (currently identified by the agency as the mausoleum of [[:en:Empress Kōgyoku|Empress Saimei]]) as the burial site of Ōta no Himemiko. The agency cites discrepancies between the archaeological findings and the Nihon Shoki's account, which states that [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] did not impose forced labor on the populace to construct the stone chamber.<ref>[http://mainichi.jp/enta/art/news/20101210ddm012040038000c.html "Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun: Discovered as Described in the Nihon Shoki; Debate Over Imperial Mausoleum Designations Accelerates"], Mainichi Shimbun, December 10, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' - In 2014 (Heisei 26), the previously designated Historic Site "Kengoshizuka Kofun" was expanded to include the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, and the designation was renamed "Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun."<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1945|牽牛子塚古墳・越塚御門古墳}}</ref>
== References ==

Based on descriptions in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], it is believed to be the probable burial site of [[:en:Princess Ōta|Princess Ōta]], a member of the Imperial family from the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]].

Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Response from the Imperial Household Agency == Despite the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has not revised its designation of the tumulus near the {{ill|Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja|車木ケンノウ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (currently identified by the agency as the mausoleum of [[:en:Empress Kōgyoku|Empress Saimei]]) as the burial site of Ōta no Himemiko. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' - In 2014 (Heisei 26), the previously designated Historic Site "Kengoshizuka Kofun" was expanded to include the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, and the designation was renamed "Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun."<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1945|牽牛子塚古墳・越塚御門古墳}}</ref>

[[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11636455}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun]][[ja:越塚御門古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11636455]] Wikidata name: Koshitsuka Gomon Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11636455]] Wikidata name: Koshitsuka Gomon Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun Stone Chamber}}]] '''Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' (越塚御門古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It has been designated a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}.
The discovery of the tumulus was announced on {{ill|December 9, 2010|JA|12月9日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 9, 2010|1=December 9|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2304}}, and it is situated adjacent to the [[:en:Kengoshizuka Kofun|Kengoshizuka Kofun]]. Based on descriptions in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], it is believed to be the probable burial site of [[:en:Princess Ōta|Princess Ōta]], a member of the Imperial family from the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]].
== Discovery == [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] In mid-October 2010, during the process of backfilling the excavation site around the Kengoshizuka Kofun, {{ill|Shinji Saikō|ja|西光慎治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji Saikō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a technician with the Asuka Village Board of Education, noticed a one-meter stone buried approximately 20 meters southeast of the Kengoshizuka Kofun. The stone showed clear evidence of human modification, prompting further excavation that led to the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20101214104644/http://www.iza.ne.jp/news/newsarticle/natnews/topics/472754/ "Unexpected Major Discovery Near Kengoshizuka Kofun"], Sankei Shimbun, December 9, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Response from the Imperial Household Agency == Despite the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has not revised its designation of the tumulus near the {{ill|Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja|車木ケンノウ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (currently identified by the agency as the mausoleum of [[:en:Empress Kōgyoku|Empress Saimei]]) as the burial site of Ōta no Himemiko. The agency cites discrepancies between the archaeological findings and the Nihon Shoki's account, which states that [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] did not impose forced labor on the populace to construct the stone chamber.<ref>[http://mainichi.jp/enta/art/news/20101210ddm012040038000c.html "Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun: Discovered as Described in the Nihon Shoki; Debate Over Imperial Mausoleum Designations Accelerates"], Mainichi Shimbun, December 10, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' - In 2014 (Heisei 26), the previously designated Historic Site "Kengoshizuka Kofun" was expanded to include the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, and the designation was renamed "Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun."<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1945|牽牛子塚古墳・越塚御門古墳}}</ref>

It has been designated a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}.
The discovery of the tumulus was announced on {{ill|December 9, 2010|JA|12月9日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 9, 2010|1=December 9|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2304}}, and it is situated adjacent to the [[:en:Kengoshizuka Kofun|Kengoshizuka Kofun]]. Based on descriptions in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], it is believed to be the probable burial site of [[:en:Princess Ōta|Princess Ōta]], a member of the Imperial family from the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]].
== Discovery == [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] In mid-October 2010, during the process of backfilling the excavation site around the Kengoshizuka Kofun, {{ill|Shinji Saikō|ja|西光慎治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji Saikō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a technician with the Asuka Village Board of Education, noticed a one-meter stone buried approximately 20 meters southeast of the Kengoshizuka Kofun. The stone showed clear evidence of human modification, prompting further excavation that led to the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20101214104644/http://www.iza.ne.jp/news/newsarticle/natnews/topics/472754/ "Unexpected Major Discovery Near Kengoshizuka Kofun"], Sankei Shimbun, December 9, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Response from the Imperial Household Agency == Despite the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has not revised its designation of the tumulus near the {{ill|Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja|車木ケンノウ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (currently identified by the agency as the mausoleum of [[:en:Empress Kōgyoku|Empress Saimei]]) as the burial site of Ōta no Himemiko. The agency cites discrepancies between the archaeological findings and the Nihon Shoki's account, which states that [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] did not impose forced labor on the populace to construct the stone chamber.<ref>[http://mainichi.jp/enta/art/news/20101210ddm012040038000c.html "Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun: Discovered as Described in the Nihon Shoki; Debate Over Imperial Mausoleum Designations Accelerates"], Mainichi Shimbun, December 10, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' - In 2014 (Heisei 26), the previously designated Historic Site "Kengoshizuka Kofun" was expanded to include the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, and the designation was renamed "Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun."<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1945|牽牛子塚古墳・越塚御門古墳}}</ref>

[[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11636455}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun]][[ja:越塚御門古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun]][[ja:越塚御門古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun]][[ja:越塚御門古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11636455]] Wikidata name: Koshitsuka Gomon Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11636455]] Wikidata name: Koshitsuka Gomon Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun Stone Chamber}}]] '''Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' (越塚御門古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It has been designated a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}.
The discovery of the tumulus was announced on {{ill|December 9, 2010|JA|12月9日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 9, 2010|1=December 9|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2304}}, and it is situated adjacent to the [[:en:Kengoshizuka Kofun|Kengoshizuka Kofun]]. Based on descriptions in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], it is believed to be the probable burial site of [[:en:Princess Ōta|Princess Ōta]], a member of the Imperial family from the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]].
== Discovery == [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] In mid-October 2010, during the process of backfilling the excavation site around the Kengoshizuka Kofun, {{ill|Shinji Saikō|ja|西光慎治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji Saikō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a technician with the Asuka Village Board of Education, noticed a one-meter stone buried approximately 20 meters southeast of the Kengoshizuka Kofun. The stone showed clear evidence of human modification, prompting further excavation that led to the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20101214104644/http://www.iza.ne.jp/news/newsarticle/natnews/topics/472754/ "Unexpected Major Discovery Near Kengoshizuka Kofun"], Sankei Shimbun, December 9, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Response from the Imperial Household Agency == Despite the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has not revised its designation of the tumulus near the {{ill|Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja|車木ケンノウ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (currently identified by the agency as the mausoleum of [[:en:Empress Kōgyoku|Empress Saimei]]) as the burial site of Ōta no Himemiko. The agency cites discrepancies between the archaeological findings and the Nihon Shoki's account, which states that [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] did not impose forced labor on the populace to construct the stone chamber.<ref>[http://mainichi.jp/enta/art/news/20101210ddm012040038000c.html "Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun: Discovered as Described in the Nihon Shoki; Debate Over Imperial Mausoleum Designations Accelerates"], Mainichi Shimbun, December 10, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' - In 2014 (Heisei 26), the previously designated Historic Site "Kengoshizuka Kofun" was expanded to include the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, and the designation was renamed "Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun."<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1945|牽牛子塚古墳・越塚御門古墳}}</ref>

It has been designated a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}.
The discovery of the tumulus was announced on {{ill|December 9, 2010|JA|12月9日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 9, 2010|1=December 9|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2304}}, and it is situated adjacent to the [[:en:Kengoshizuka Kofun|Kengoshizuka Kofun]]. Based on descriptions in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], it is believed to be the probable burial site of [[:en:Princess Ōta|Princess Ōta]], a member of the Imperial family from the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]].
== Discovery == [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] In mid-October 2010, during the process of backfilling the excavation site around the Kengoshizuka Kofun, {{ill|Shinji Saikō|ja|西光慎治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji Saikō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a technician with the Asuka Village Board of Education, noticed a one-meter stone buried approximately 20 meters southeast of the Kengoshizuka Kofun. The stone showed clear evidence of human modification, prompting further excavation that led to the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20101214104644/http://www.iza.ne.jp/news/newsarticle/natnews/topics/472754/ "Unexpected Major Discovery Near Kengoshizuka Kofun"], Sankei Shimbun, December 9, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Response from the Imperial Household Agency == Despite the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has not revised its designation of the tumulus near the {{ill|Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja|車木ケンノウ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (currently identified by the agency as the mausoleum of [[:en:Empress Kōgyoku|Empress Saimei]]) as the burial site of Ōta no Himemiko. The agency cites discrepancies between the archaeological findings and the Nihon Shoki's account, which states that [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] did not impose forced labor on the populace to construct the stone chamber.<ref>[http://mainichi.jp/enta/art/news/20101210ddm012040038000c.html "Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun: Discovered as Described in the Nihon Shoki; Debate Over Imperial Mausoleum Designations Accelerates"], Mainichi Shimbun, December 10, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' - In 2014 (Heisei 26), the previously designated Historic Site "Kengoshizuka Kofun" was expanded to include the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, and the designation was renamed "Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun."<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1945|牽牛子塚古墳・越塚御門古墳}}</ref>

Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Response from the Imperial Household Agency == Despite the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has not revised its designation of the tumulus near the {{ill|Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja|車木ケンノウ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (currently identified by the agency as the mausoleum of [[:en:Empress Kōgyoku|Empress Saimei]]) as the burial site of Ōta no Himemiko. The agency cites discrepancies between the archaeological findings and the Nihon Shoki's account, which states that [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] did not impose forced labor on the populace to construct the stone chamber.<ref>[http://mainichi.jp/enta/art/news/20101210ddm012040038000c.html "Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun: Discovered as Described in the Nihon Shoki; Debate Over Imperial Mausoleum Designations Accelerates"], Mainichi Shimbun, December 10, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' - In 2014 (Heisei 26), the previously designated Historic Site "Kengoshizuka Kofun" was expanded to include the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, and the designation was renamed "Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun."<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1945|牽牛子塚古墳・越塚御門古墳}}</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun]][[ja:越塚御門古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun]][[ja:越塚御門古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun]][[ja:越塚御門古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11636455]] Wikidata name: Koshitsuka Gomon Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11636455]] Wikidata name: Koshitsuka Gomon Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun Stone Chamber}}]] '''Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' (越塚御門古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It has been designated a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}.
The discovery of the tumulus was announced on {{ill|December 9, 2010|JA|12月9日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 9, 2010|1=December 9|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2304}}, and it is situated adjacent to the [[:en:Kengoshizuka Kofun|Kengoshizuka Kofun]]. Based on descriptions in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], it is believed to be the probable burial site of [[:en:Princess Ōta|Princess Ōta]], a member of the Imperial family from the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]].
== Discovery == [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] In mid-October 2010, during the process of backfilling the excavation site around the Kengoshizuka Kofun, {{ill|Shinji Saikō|ja|西光慎治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji Saikō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a technician with the Asuka Village Board of Education, noticed a one-meter stone buried approximately 20 meters southeast of the Kengoshizuka Kofun. The stone showed clear evidence of human modification, prompting further excavation that led to the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20101214104644/http://www.iza.ne.jp/news/newsarticle/natnews/topics/472754/ "Unexpected Major Discovery Near Kengoshizuka Kofun"], Sankei Shimbun, December 9, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Response from the Imperial Household Agency == Despite the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has not revised its designation of the tumulus near the {{ill|Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja|車木ケンノウ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (currently identified by the agency as the mausoleum of [[:en:Empress Kōgyoku|Empress Saimei]]) as the burial site of Ōta no Himemiko. The agency cites discrepancies between the archaeological findings and the Nihon Shoki's account, which states that [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] did not impose forced labor on the populace to construct the stone chamber.<ref>[http://mainichi.jp/enta/art/news/20101210ddm012040038000c.html "Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun: Discovered as Described in the Nihon Shoki; Debate Over Imperial Mausoleum Designations Accelerates"], Mainichi Shimbun, December 10, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' - In 2014 (Heisei 26), the previously designated Historic Site "Kengoshizuka Kofun" was expanded to include the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, and the designation was renamed "Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun."<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1945|牽牛子塚古墳・越塚御門古墳}}</ref>

[[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun Stone Chamber}}]] '''Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' (越塚御門古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. It has been designated a {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
The discovery of the tumulus was announced on {{ill|December 9, 2010|JA|12月9日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 9, 2010|1=December 9|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2304}}, and it is situated adjacent to the {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|en|Kengoshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11571102|lt=Kengoshizuka Kofun}}. Based on descriptions in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, it is believed to be the probable burial site of {{ill|Princess Ōta|en|Princess Ōta|WD=Q603057|lt=Princess Ōta}}, a member of the Imperial family from the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}.
== Discovery == [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] In mid-October 2010, during the process of backfilling the excavation site around the Kengoshizuka Kofun, {{ill|Shinji Saikō|ja|西光慎治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji Saikō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a technician with the Asuka Village Board of Education, noticed a one-meter stone buried approximately 20 meters southeast of the Kengoshizuka Kofun. The stone showed clear evidence of human modification, prompting further excavation that led to the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20101214104644/http://www.iza.ne.jp/news/newsarticle/natnews/topics/472754/ "Unexpected Major Discovery Near Kengoshizuka Kofun"], Sankei Shimbun, December 9, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Response from the Imperial Household Agency == Despite the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} has not revised its designation of the tumulus near the {{ill|Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja|車木ケンノウ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (currently identified by the agency as the mausoleum of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|en|Empress Kōgyoku|WD=Q235296|lt=Empress Saimei}}) as the burial site of Ōta no Himemiko. The agency cites discrepancies between the archaeological findings and the Nihon Shoki's account, which states that {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}} did not impose forced labor on the populace to construct the stone chamber.<ref>[http://mainichi.jp/enta/art/news/20101210ddm012040038000c.html "Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun: Discovered as Described in the Nihon Shoki; Debate Over Imperial Mausoleum Designations Accelerates"], Mainichi Shimbun, December 10, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' - In 2014 (Heisei 26), the previously designated Historic Site "Kengoshizuka Kofun" was expanded to include the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, and the designation was renamed "Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun."<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1945|牽牛子塚古墳・越塚御門古墳}}</ref>
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1945|牽牛子塚古墳・越塚御門古墳}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
越塚御門古墳]]
{{translated page|ja|越塚御門古墳|version=101914259|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:越塚御門古墳]]

== Response from the Imperial Household Agency == Despite the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has not revised its designation of the tumulus near the {{ill|Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja|車木ケンノウ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (currently identified by the agency as the mausoleum of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|en|Empress Kōgyoku|WD=Q235296|lt=Empress Saimei|1=Empress Kōgyoku}}) as the burial site of Ōta no Himemiko. The agency cites discrepancies between the archaeological findings and the Nihon Shoki's account, which states that {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}} did not impose forced labor on the populace to construct the stone chamber.<ref>[http://mainichi.jp/enta/art/news/20101210ddm012040038000c.html "Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun: Discovered as Described in the Nihon Shoki; Debate Over Imperial Mausoleum Designations Accelerates"], Mainichi Shimbun, December 10, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' - In 2014 (Heisei 26), the previously designated Historic Site "Kengoshizuka Kofun" was expanded to include the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, and the designation was renamed "Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun."<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1945|牽牛子塚古墳・越塚御門古墳}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun]][[ja:越塚御門古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun]][[ja:越塚御門古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun]][[ja:越塚御門古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}}}
[[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun Stone Chamber}}]] '''Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' (越塚御門古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka, Nara|1=Asuka, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. It has been designated a {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}}.
The discovery of the tumulus was announced on {{ill|December 9, 2010|JA|12月9日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 9, 2010|1=December 9|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2304}}, and it is situated adjacent to the {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|en|Kengoshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11571102|lt=Kengoshizuka Kofun|1=Kengoshizuka Kofun}}. Based on descriptions in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, it is believed to be the probable burial site of {{ill|Princess Ōta|en|Princess Ōta|WD=Q603057|lt=Princess Ōta|1=Princess Ōta}}, a member of the Imperial family from the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}}.
== Discovery == [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] In mid-October 2010, during the process of backfilling the excavation site around the Kengoshizuka Kofun, {{ill|Shinji Saikō|ja|西光慎治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji Saikō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a technician with the Asuka Village Board of Education, noticed a one-meter stone buried approximately 20 meters southeast of the Kengoshizuka Kofun. The stone showed clear evidence of human modification, prompting further excavation that led to the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20101214104644/http://www.iza.ne.jp/news/newsarticle/natnews/topics/472754/ "Unexpected Major Discovery Near Kengoshizuka Kofun"], Sankei Shimbun, December 9, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Response from the Imperial Household Agency == Despite the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has not revised its designation of the tumulus near the {{ill|Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja|車木ケンノウ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (currently identified by the agency as the mausoleum of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|en|Empress Kōgyoku|WD=Q235296|lt=Empress Saimei|1=Empress Kōgyoku}}) as the burial site of Ōta no Himemiko. The agency cites discrepancies between the archaeological findings and the Nihon Shoki's account, which states that {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}} did not impose forced labor on the populace to construct the stone chamber.<ref>[http://mainichi.jp/enta/art/news/20101210ddm012040038000c.html "Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun: Discovered as Described in the Nihon Shoki; Debate Over Imperial Mausoleum Designations Accelerates"], Mainichi Shimbun, December 10, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' - In 2014 (Heisei 26), the previously designated Historic Site "Kengoshizuka Kofun" was expanded to include the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, and the designation was renamed "Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun."<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1945|牽牛子塚古墳・越塚御門古墳}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun]][[ja:越塚御門古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun]][[ja:越塚御門古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun]][[ja:越塚御門古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Asuka|qid=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}}}
[[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun Stone Chamber}}]] '''Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' (越塚御門古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|qid=Q752397|lt=Asuka, Nara|1=Asuka, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. It has been designated a {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}}.
The discovery of the tumulus was announced on {{ill|December 9, 2010|JA|12月9日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 9, 2010|1=December 9|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2304}}, and it is situated adjacent to the {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|en|Kengoshizuka Kofun|qid=Q11571102|lt=Kengoshizuka Kofun|1=Kengoshizuka Kofun}}. Based on descriptions in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, it is believed to be the probable burial site of {{ill|Princess Ōta|en|Princess Ōta|qid=Q603057|lt=Princess Ōta|1=Princess Ōta}}, a member of the Imperial family from the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}}.
== Discovery == [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] In mid-October 2010, during the process of backfilling the excavation site around the Kengoshizuka Kofun, {{ill|Shinji Saikō|ja|西光慎治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji Saikō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a technician with the Asuka Village Board of Education, noticed a one-meter stone buried approximately 20 meters southeast of the Kengoshizuka Kofun. The stone showed clear evidence of human modification, prompting further excavation that led to the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20101214104644/http://www.iza.ne.jp/news/newsarticle/natnews/topics/472754/ "Unexpected Major Discovery Near Kengoshizuka Kofun"], Sankei Shimbun, December 9, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Response from the Imperial Household Agency == Despite the discovery of the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has not revised its designation of the tumulus near the {{ill|Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja|車木ケンノウ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramoki no Misasagi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (currently identified by the agency as the mausoleum of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|en|Empress Kōgyoku|qid=Q235296|lt=Empress Saimei|1=Empress Kōgyoku}}) as the burial site of Ōta no Himemiko. The agency cites discrepancies between the archaeological findings and the Nihon Shoki's account, which states that {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|qid=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}} did not impose forced labor on the populace to construct the stone chamber.<ref>[http://mainichi.jp/enta/art/news/20101210ddm012040038000c.html "Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun: Discovered as Described in the Nihon Shoki; Debate Over Imperial Mausoleum Designations Accelerates"], Mainichi Shimbun, December 10, 2010. Accessed December 2010.</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun''' - In 2014 (Heisei 26), the previously designated Historic Site "Kengoshizuka Kofun" was expanded to include the Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, and the designation was renamed "Kengoshizuka Kofun and Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun."<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1945|牽牛子塚古墳・越塚御門古墳}}</ref>
